# Map & de-friction the customer journey (signposts, risk-removal, friction)
Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon supplements brand, plateaued at $4M with conversion slipping on her hero SKU (ASIN B0SUP0001, a magnesium-glycinate sleep formula). She wants her customer journey mapped to the five signpost stages, the conversion leaks named, and a fix list — then exported as a doc her team can execute against. No theory.

**Maya (User):** Conversion's slipping on B0SUP0001 and I can feel it's a journey problem, not a copy problem — people land and don't move. Map my journey to the five stages, tell me which Signpost emotion I'm whiffing at each, and give me a friction/risk-removal fix list. I know the funnel — don't define "awareness" for me. Just run it on MY listing and DTC PDP.

**Coach:** On it — here's the answer first, then the map. Customers never buy randomly; each move between stages is pulled by an emotional Signpost, so a stall means a Signpost you're not fulfilling. I'll place your assets on the five stages, name the emotion each needs, and flag where you're silent.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/01-the-five-stages-and-signposts/00-the-five-stage-funnel.md⟧ ⟦iv-os: read-product-truth (B0SUP0001 specs + per-avatar benefits)⟧ ⟦iv-os: read-canon (get_positioning / get_messaging_pillars)⟧

**Coach:** ⟦tool: run_conversation {message: "map B0SUP0001 journey to the five signpost stages, flag conversion leaks", session_context: "B0SUP0001 Amazon listing + DTC PDP, $34, conversion slipping"}⟧ → your Signpost map, stage by stage (emotion → does an asset fulfill it):

| Stage | Signpost emotion needed | Your asset today | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Awareness** | Curiosity / frustration | Sponsored ad + organic title "Magnesium Glycinate 400mg" | **Whiff** — leads with the molecule, not the frustration. No one's curiosity/frustration is named. |
| **Consideration** | Hope / anticipation | Bullets list ingredients + dose | **Weak** — specs, no hope. Buyer can't picture feeling better. |
| **Decision** | Trust / relief (+ Shopper High) | Star rating + price | **Leak** — no testimonial proof, no plainly-stated guarantee. Trust unearned at the exact moment they want relief. |
| **Retention** | Satisfaction / validation | Standard "shipped" email | **Silent** — nothing reinforces "you made the right call." |
| **Advocacy** | Pride / belonging | None | **Silent** — no prompt or reason to share. |

The two emotions you're whiffing hardest are **Awareness (curiosity/frustration)** and **Decision (trust/relief)** — that's your stall. The map: each Signpost is the feeling that moves them to the next stage; a stage with no Signpost-addressing move is where customers park.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/01-the-five-stages-and-signposts/02-the-signposts-per-stage.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Decision is where I'm bleeding — they add to cart and bounce. I get "trust." What's the actual fix, not "build trust." And don't tell me the awareness ad copy, that's a separate problem.

**Coach:** Decision Signpost = trust/relief, and the proven move is **risk removal** — customers fear wasting money on a supplement that won't work, so you lower perceived risk at the cart. For your category the dominant fear is *spend anxiety* ("another $34 pill that does nothing"), which the money-back/satisfaction-guarantee category disarms. Concretely:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/02-removing-anxiety-and-risk/01-risk-removal-strategies.md⟧

- **Satisfaction-or-money-back, stated plainly at the cart** — the Chewy/Headspace pattern (full refund if not satisfied in 30 days). Disarms "what if it doesn't work for me."
- **Testimonial proof at Decision** — the travel-company pattern: reviews + a clear refund policy turn trust into relief right when they're choosing.

State the guarantee in plain words *on the Decision asset* (PDP cart area / Amazon A+), not buried in an FAQ. That's the relief that lets them anticipate the Shopper High instead of bouncing.

**Maya (User):** We have a 30-day money-back — it's just not on the PDP, it's in the policy page. So that's a placement fix. What else is leaking? Walk the whole path and give me frictions, not just Decision.

**Coach:** Right — you *have* the risk-remover, it's just not at the Signpost, so it isn't working. That's a placement fix, logged. Now the friction pass — walking your path stage by stage, each friction matched to the proven removal pattern:   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/03-timing-and-friction/03-addressing-friction-points.md⟧

| Stage | Friction (your path) | Proven removal pattern |
|---|---|---|
| Consideration | Specs-only bullets; buyer can't tell if it's for *them* | Anticipate the doubt — Warby-Parker "see it through their eyes": name the exact problem ("wake at 3am?") so they self-select. |
| Decision | Guarantee hidden on policy page | Airbnb transparent-pricing pattern: surface the total reassurance up front, not buried. |
| Decision | Checkout has extra steps on DTC | Amazon one-click / Apple-Pay autofill pattern: cut steps, reduce cart abandonment. |
| Post-purchase | No order tracking signal | Domino's tracker pattern: real-time transparency kills wait-time anxiety. |
| Retention | "Shipped" email does no reinforcing | (Signpost move, below) personalized validation. |

And the deeper Awareness/Consideration blocker is the **"habit of the present"** — a melatonin user isn't anxious about your dose, they're anxious about *switching off a pill they already trust*. Frame magnesium as familiar-and-better ("same bedtime routine, no morning grog"), not a leap.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/02-removing-anxiety-and-risk/00-overcoming-anxiety-habit-of-the-present.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/03-timing-and-friction/04-anticipating-barriers.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Good — the switching-anxiety read is right, that's exactly the melatonin objection. One more: is there a timing play? Q4 is coming and people sleep worse over the holidays.

**Coach:** Yes — context-and-timing: a product's value is how well it fits the customer's life *at that moment*, and the holidays are a need-spike for sleep (stress, travel, disrupted routine). "Sensitive timing is everything." The play is a context-led Awareness message timed to that moment — name the holiday sleep-wreck, then the offer. That makes the switch feel necessary, not optional.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/03-timing-and-friction/00-context-and-timing.md⟧

**Maya (User):** That's the whole map. Export it — Signpost map plus the fix list, as a doc my team can run against. PDF.

**Coach:** Exporting the journey section — Signpost map + risk-removal/friction fix list, owner-ready.   ⟦tool: export_strategy_doc⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/01-the-five-stages-and-signposts/02-the-signposts-per-stage.md⟧

**Coach:** ⟦tool: export_strategy_doc {section: "customer_journey", format: "pdf", brand_context: "B0SUP0001 sleep formula, $34", avatar_context: "3am-waker, distrusts melatonin"}⟧ → `journey-map_B0SUP0001.pdf` generated (Signpost map + fix list below).   ⟦iv-os: write-produced-asset (log_asset → strategy doc, request_id returned)⟧

**Your one next move:** put the 30-day money-back guarantee in plain words on the PDP cart area + Amazon A+ today — it's your highest-leverage Decision-stage fix (the risk-remover you already own, just not where the Signpost needs it). Say the word and I'll run the same journey map on your #2 SKU.

### Artifact produced

**Per-stage Signpost map (`run_conversation`) — B0SUP0001:**
| Stage | Signpost emotion | Current asset | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| Awareness | Curiosity / frustration | Molecule-led ad + title | Whiff (frustration unnamed) |
| Consideration | Hope / anticipation | Spec/ingredient bullets | Weak (no hope, can't picture relief) |
| Decision | Trust / relief (+ Shopper High) | Rating + price | Leak (no surfaced proof or guarantee) |
| Retention | Satisfaction / validation | Generic "shipped" email | Silent |
| Advocacy | Pride / belonging | None | Silent |

**Risk-removal / friction fix list (priority order):**
1. **Decision — placement fix (P0):** existing 30-day money-back guarantee stated plainly on PDP cart + Amazon A+ (satisfaction-or-money-back pattern — Chewy/Headspace). Risk-remover already owned, just not at the Signpost.
2. **Decision:** add testimonial proof at the cart (travel-company pattern: reviews + clear refund = trust→relief).
3. **Decision:** cut DTC checkout steps (one-click / Apple-Pay autofill pattern).
4. **Consideration:** rewrite bullets to name the 3am-wake problem so buyers self-select (Warby-Parker "see it through their eyes").
5. **Awareness/Consideration:** reframe vs the "habit of the present" — magnesium as familiar-and-better than melatonin ("same routine, no morning grog"), not a leap.
6. **Post-purchase:** add order-tracking transparency (Domino's-tracker pattern).
7. **Timing:** holiday-context Awareness message (sleep-spike moment) — name the moment, then the offer.

**Exported doc (`export_strategy_doc`):** `journey-map_B0SUP0001.pdf` — `customer_journey` section, Signpost map + fix list, logged to IV-OS as an asset.

**One next action:** surface the owned 30-day guarantee on the PDP cart + A+ today (highest-leverage Decision fix).

- [persona-adapt:P1] Coach led with the answer then one-line why; compressed (no "awareness" definition — she forbade it, and the coach honored "don't tell me the awareness ad copy"); filled the five-stage map FOR her from her assets rather than handing her a blank worksheet; used HER data (B0SUP0001, $34, existing-but-buried 30-day guarantee, melatonin objection, Q4 timing); batched the map + risk + friction + timing across turns; produced a done-for-you fix list + exported doc she runs against; ended on exactly one next action. Compression + done-for-you draft both present.
- [artifact] Both deliverables present: the per-stage Signpost map (5 stages × emotion × verdict) and the prioritized risk-removal/friction fix list, plus the exported `journey-map_B0SUP0001.pdf`.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; all inputs are Maya's own (ASIN, $34, her own guarantee/email/checkout, melatonin objection) or tool outputs; IV-OS product-truth/canon reads and the asset write shown only as ⟦iv-os:⟧ capability tags — never invented as concrete records; brand examples (Chewy, Headspace, Warby Parker, Domino's, Airbnb) are the book's own verbatim illustrations from the cited skills, not fabricated stats; no invented conversion numbers or claims beyond the cited skills.
